meta.source: Structure mechanically extracted from the reference production pipeline v1.8. EN word lists that had no production baseline are authored (seeded by translating the tuned RU lists) and flagged with "_confidence": "low" until an EN tenant supplies training data.
notes: New language recipe: COPY en.pack.json to <lang>.pack.json, translate the VALUES, never delete or rename a key. The renderer warns per missing ui_strings key and the render tests assert en/ru key parity — a dropped key silently degrades the Slack strings for that tenant.

Every pack for every language MUST carry the identical key set; the tenant renderer (tenant-render.js) selects ui_strings by tenant.primary_language and UNIONS the list-valued linguistic fields (stop_words, domain_bridge, inflection_suffixes, discriminator lists, status_markers, voice_directives, feedback_patterns, sensitivity_patterns) across all tenant.languages.
Keys prefixed with an underscore are annotations for humans; the renderer ignores them.

domain_bridge / inflection_suffixes / transliteration are intentionally empty for English: the consuming helpers (expandToken, dictGet, transliterate) degrade to identity on empty tables — no code path changes for single-language tenants.

ui_strings templates use {placeholder} interpolation tokens; the renderer emits them verbatim into a per-node `const S = {...}` lookup and node code performs the substitution. Placeholders must not be renamed per-language.
sensitivity_patterns are empty here by design: English sensitivity defaults ship in tenant.schema.json; packs only CONTRIBUTE additional per-language patterns.

voice directive phrases use the literal token [TRACKER-ID] where the production prompt used a tracker-specific issue-identifier token; the prompt renderer replaces it with the tenant's tracker identifier example.

discriminator._note: Authored in W1 by translating the adversarially tuned RU lists (production 09b, validated on a 349-row labeled set). No EN production baseline exists yet — treat every list as low-confidence until an EN tenant supplies training data. Pattern LOGIC (score bands, dual gates, compound bonus) lives in code and is language-independent.

voice_directives._note: Directive phrases that follow a wake word (wake words are tenant config: extraction.voice.wake_words). EN tokens 'task'/'to-do'/'skip'/'not a task'/'update [TRACKER-ID]' are production-verbatim; the rest are authored expansions.

status_markers._note: Per-language verb cues injected into the summarizer prompt's action_items.status detection rules. 'just fixed it' / 'we did it' / "I'll do" / "we'll need to" / 'keep an eye on' are production-verbatim; the rest are authored.

feedback_patterns._note: Phrase exemplars per feedback verdict class for the feedback-parser prompt. The verdict taxonomy (already_exists / already_done / duplicate_in_batch / wrong_* / good) is the contract and lives in the prompt; exemplars are hints — classification is by meaning, so unlisted languages degrade gracefully. EN exemplars authored (no EN production baseline).

task_block_headers._note: 4-block issue-body headers (EN canon: plugin/templates/frontmatter/task-4block.md). Selected by tenant.primary_language like ui_strings — issue bodies carry the team's working language, no runtime mirroring. Keys are fixed (context / task / result / extra); the composer prompt renders them verbatim.

ui_strings._note: Code-emitted (non-LLM) Slack strings. No runtime language mirroring — the digest channel has one working language, selected by tenant.primary_language.
ui_strings.main.transcript_download_failed: > :warning:  _Transcript download failed (status {status}). Summary built from metadata only._
ui_strings.main.summary_truncated: ⚠️ Summary truncated by the model max_tokens cap (output_tokens={output_tokens}). Some action items / decisions may be missing. Raise llm.summarizer.max_tokens if recurring.
ui_strings.main.upstream_failed: :x: *Processing failed before vault commit* — an upstream step (transcript download / AI summary / parse) errored, so nothing was committed.
ui_strings.main.no_thread_root_vault_ok: :white_check_mark: Vault commit SUCCEEDED ({path}) — only the Slack posts are missing.
ui_strings.main.no_thread_root_vault_unknown: :x: Vault commit state unknown — check the n8n execution / DLQ entry.
ui_strings.tasks.planning_banner: ⚠️ _Planning mode_ — on these calls the team usually walks through issues that ALREADY exist in the tracker. Review every **CREATE** button manually — chances are the task already exists and needs a COMMENT, not a CREATE. Bulk-approve is disabled.
ui_strings.tasks.all_create_tripwire: 🚨 *0 matches across {count} proposals* — dedup almost certainly missed (matcher recall hole). Do NOT bulk-create: check the tracker first, and where an issue already exists, comment manually.
ui_strings.tasks.truncation_note: ⚠️ +{count} proposals not shown (Slack's 50-block limit). Handle the visible ones (or «Skip all remaining»); the full list is in the thread/vault.
ui_strings.tasks.footer: _Click Approve → real write to the tracker (idempotent). Skip → log only._
ui_strings.tasks.same_target_dup_note: _both proposals target this issue; task #{task_id} was chosen_
ui_strings.tasks.bulk_noop: _Nothing left to execute — all tasks already handled._
ui_strings.tasks.tracker_forbidden_member:
⚠️ Tracker refused: `{assignee}` is not a member of the target team. 
Options: (a) add `{assignee}` to that team in the tracker → retry; (b) tell me to create the task in a team where `{assignee}` is a member; (c) create it manually with the right team.
ui_strings.tasks.cannot_create_no_team: Cannot create a new task: the router could not resolve a team for «{title}». Possible causes: assignee_hint='{owner}' is not a member of any known team, or the matcher produced no alt payload. Create the task manually or tell me the team explicitly.
ui_strings.tasks.cannot_create_no_team_short: ⚠️ Cannot create «{title}» — no team for assignee_hint='{owner}'.
ui_strings.tasks.update_not_applied: ⚠️ Could not apply the update: {warnings}. Update the status manually in the tracker.
ui_strings.disc.planning_score_token: Planning + score {score} + «{token}» — possibly an existing issue
ui_strings.disc.planning_score: Planning + score {score} — possibly an existing issue
ui_strings.disc.long_planning: Long planning call — duplicate risk among CREATEs
ui_strings.disc.urgent_no_deadline: urgent priority without a deadline in the title
ui_strings.disc.flag_uncertain: FLAG with score {score} — the matcher is unsure
ui_strings.disc.create_without_match: Long dev sync — a CREATE without a match is suspicious
ui_strings.disc.call_to_decide: A call scheduled to make a decision («{sched}»+«{decide}») — not a trackable task
ui_strings.disc.chat_resolve: «{token}» — gets resolved in chat, not a separate task
ui_strings.dlq.retry_hint: _Run redrive-dlq.js on this DLQ entry — restores the artifact. Or n8n UI → "Retry from failed node"._
ui_strings.feedback.digest_header: :bar_chart: *Backbrief · tasks feedback digest* (auto-collected from thread replies)
